$comment: D-FFI-TCL-DESCRIPTOR (SQLite testfixture arc, C1 2026-07-17): the neutral <tcl.h> FFI descriptor. SQLite's `testfixture` (the binary that runs the 1190-file .test suite = 'all sqlite units') is ~90 dense C TUs linked against libtcl8.6, and every src/test*.c uses TCL's C API. testfixture calls TCL DIRECTLY — its build recipe (main.mk `make -n testfixture`) carries NO -DUSE_TCL_STUBS (that flag is only on the loadable-EXTENSION targets tclsqlite-{shell,stubs}.o), and /usr/include/tcl8.6/tcl.h has ZERO tclStubsPtr references — so these are plain extern prototypes, NOT the stubs-table macro form. That makes tcl.json an ORDINARY (if large) FFI descriptor, exactly like the libc ones. | LINKAGE: the elf `library` is libtcl8.6.so (TCL's SONAME — it ships an UNVERSIONED soname; `readelf -d libtcl8.6.so.0` -> SONAME libtcl8.6.so, and the runtime pkg libtcl8.6 ships BOTH libtcl8.6.so and the libtcl8.6.so.0 compat file, so the soname resolves without tcl8.6-dev). The ELF walker emits one DT_NEEDED per DISTINCT referenced import library (D-FFI-MATH-LIBM-DT-NEEDED, the libm.so.6 precedent), so a <tcl.h> user links libtcl8.6.so ALONGSIDE libc.so.6 — witnessed by examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_eval (RUN -> exit 42 on elf x86_64). | availableObjectFormats:[elf] — TCL exists on pe/macho too but with different sonames + no run witness yet; a pe/macho <tcl.h> include fails loud until those legs land (later arc cycle). | OPAQUE HANDLES: Tcl_Interp/Tcl_Obj are modeled like stdio.json's FILE — a named struct with an opaque byte blob, used only via ptr<...> (never by-value), so member access fails loud and the blob size is inert. C1 shipped the eval surface (5 functions); C2 (2026-07-17) adds the COMMAND-REGISTRATION surface (Tcl_NewIntObj/Tcl_SetObjResult + Tcl_CreateObjCommand, whose 3rd param is a FUNCTION POINTER `ptr<fn(...)>` — the signal.json handler precedent) so a C command can be registered and CALLED BACK by libtcl — the core shape of every SQLite test*.c command (witnessed by examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_command → 42). C4 (2026-07-17) adds the SCALAR/STRING/RESULT surface a real test TU uses: the ClientData typedef (void*), the TCL_OK/TCL_ERROR/TCL_STATIC/NULL PP-macros (the `macros` surface — signal.json's `#ifdef`-safe precedent), and Tcl_GetString/Tcl_WrongNumArgs/Tcl_SetResult/Tcl_AppendResult (the last VARIADIC, the stdio printf-family precedent) — witnessed by examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_strcmd (a string-arg command → 42) and grounded by src/test_backup.c's error count dropping (34 → the next Tcl_CmdInfo-introspection layer). C5 (2026-07-17) adds the COMMAND-INTROSPECTION layer: the Tcl_CmdInfo struct (the FIRST field-access struct here — 8 fields, int + 7 pointers, modeled via the `structs` surface so `cmdInfo.deleteProc` resolves + libtcl fills it at the ABI-correct offsets) + Tcl_GetCommandInfo/Tcl_SetCommandInfo/Tcl_DeleteCommand/Tcl_GetIndexFromObjStruct — witnessed by examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_cmdinfo (registers a command, introspects it via Tcl_GetCommandInfo, checks isNativeObjectProc==1 [a runtime layout witness] → 42) and grounded by test_backup.c reaching a CLEAN compile (0 errors). 16 functions total; the full 106-symbol test-TU surface + the real Tcl_Obj layout + CONST/Tcl_Size land as later cycles' real test-TU consumers demand them. C13 (2026-07-18) unblocks the REFCOUNT MACROS (dissolves D-FFI-TCL-REFCOUNT-MACROS — the dominant post-C9 S0001 test-TU blocker: test_bestindex/test_func/test_mutex use Tcl_IncrRefCount, test_tclvar uses Tcl_DecrRefCount, test8 uses Tcl_DStringValue). Real tcl.h's Tcl_IncrRefCount/Tcl_DecrRefCount are field-poking macros over Tcl_Obj->refCount, but libtcl8.6.so EXPORTS Tcl_DbIncrRefCount/Tcl_DbDecrRefCount (nm -D) doing the same ++/--refCount (+free) — so they are modeled as function-macros over those EXPORTED funcs (the ckalloc/ckfree precedent), needing NO Tcl_Obj named-field layout (Tcl_Obj stays the opaque 6xu64 blob) and thus NO ~30-signature ripple; the empty-string + 0 file/line args are the harmless Db* debug-build params. Tcl_DStringValue(dsPtr) -> ((dsPtr)->string) DOES need a field, so Tcl_DString gains its REAL named layout (char* string; int length; int spaceAvl; char staticSpace[200] = 216B, same size/align as the old opaque arr<u64,27> blob) via the structs surface (the Tcl_CmdInfo C5 precedent) + every Tcl_DString* signature updated to the field body. Also +Tcl_GetSlave/Tcl_BackgroundError (test-TU S0001 residual). The 4 Db*/Slave/BgError syms are EXPORTED, so DSS's eager-import-all-descriptor-fns stays safe here (unlike the C9 macro-only refcount attempt that broke every tcl binary's LOAD). Witnessed by examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_refcount (RUN -> 42 on elf x86_64). C27 (2026-07-20) adds Tcl_ObjGetVar2 (`Tcl_Obj *Tcl_ObjGetVar2(Tcl_Interp*, Tcl_Obj*, Tcl_Obj*, int)`, tclDecls.h:590 — the GET twin of the already-shipped Tcl_ObjSetVar2, one fewer param [no newValuePtr]) for sqlite src/test_quota.c:16087 `Tcl_ObjGetVar2(p->interp, pVarname, 0, 0)`, taking test_quota.c CLEAN (corpus 37 -> 38). Exported `T Tcl_ObjGetVar2` (nm -D libtcl8.6.so, mirrors the shipped `T Tcl_ObjSetVar2`), so eager-import stays safe. The stubs `#define Tcl_ObjGetVar2 (tclStubsPtr->tcl_ObjGetVar2)` is inside the USE_TCL_STUBS block testfixture never sets, so the plain extern applies (the whole-descriptor invariant). Witnessed by examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_getvar2 (sets a real interp var v=42, reads it back via Tcl_ObjGetVar2 -> 42 on elf x86_64). C28 (2026-07-20) grows the test1.c (the keystone command-registration TU) surface with 6 need-driven, PURE-descriptor items — 3 object-macro constants + 1 field-macro over an EXISTING field + 2 exported functions: TCL_EVAL_DIRECT=0x040000 (262144), TCL_LEAVE_ERR_MSG=0x200 (512), TCL_LINK_STRING=4 (tcl.h:1079/1108/1153; used at test1.c:25561 Tcl_EvalObjEx flags / :29874 Tcl_GetVar2Ex flags / :9404 Tcl_LinkVar type) modeled in the `macros` surface like every other TCL_* constant; Tcl_DStringLength(dsPtr) -> ((dsPtr)->length) (tcl.h:1014) — the LENGTH twin of the already-shipped Tcl_DStringValue macro, resolving against the Tcl_DString `length` field already modeled in C13 (NO new struct layout); Tcl_GetDouble(Tcl_Interp*, const char*, double*) -> int (tclDecls.h:146) the Tcl_GetInt double-out sibling, and Tcl_DStringAppend(Tcl_DString*, const char*, int) -> char* (tclDecls.h:382) the Tcl_DStringAppendElement sibling. BOTH functions are exported `T` (nm -D libtcl8.6.so: Tcl_GetDouble @0xce230, Tcl_DStringAppend @0x12a5c0), so DSS's eager-import-all path stays safe. Witnessed by examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_dstring (parses "30" via Tcl_GetDouble + builds a 12-char DString via Tcl_DStringAppend, reads its length via the Tcl_DStringLength macro, pins the 3 constant values -> 30+12=42 on elf x86_64). test1.c ADVANCES but is NOT yet CLEAN: it still hits (1) the real Tcl_Obj field layout — test1.c:29876 `pVar->typePtr` / :29877 `pVar->typePtr->name` read fields of the opaque Tcl_Obj (arr<u64,6>) blob + the Tcl_ObjType struct, the explicitly-deferred C13-scale Tcl_Obj-layout cycle; (2) test1.c:31546 `cmdInfo.objProc(...)` CALLS through Tcl_CmdInfo.objProc, which is modeled ptr<void> — a fn-ptr field-type refinement (a coordinated typedef+structs+2-signature edit, since the interner is structural) deferred to its own witnessed cycle; (3) the non-tcl unistd _SC_NPROCESSORS_ONLN (test1.c:9033 sysconf arg) -> a unistd.json constant follow-up. C32 (2026-07-20) adds the `includes` surface (D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-INCLUDES): the real /usr/include/tcl8.6/tcl.h transitively `#include`s <stdio.h> (line 125), <stdarg.h> (138), <limits.h> (422, guarded). A TU reaching tcl.json via `#include <tcl.h>` (test_md5.c through tclsqlite.h) therefore ALSO needs <stdio.h>'s surface (FILE/fopen/fread/fseek/fclose/SEEK_SET) — which a FLAT descriptor cannot carry. `includes:["stdio.h"]` models that edge: resolving <tcl.h> for a TU ALSO resolves + injects stdio.json's surface, via the shared cycle-safe closure walker (both the preprocessor macro-splice AND the import-resolver typed-surface record walk the SAME transitive set). NEED-DRIVEN + MINIMAL: only stdio.h has a consumer today (test_md5's FILE path); stdarg.h (a documentary va_list the builtin shadows) + limits.h (guarded in real tcl.h) are real edges DEFERRED until a consumer needs them, to bound the blast radius. stdio.json is availability-neutral (available on every format), so tcl->stdio is safe everywhere (on pe/macho the <tcl.h> availability gate already fails loud first). Witnessed by examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_transitive_stdio (opens a file via FILE/fopen/fread/fclose arriving ONLY through tcl.json->stdio.h, no direct <stdio.h> -> exit 42 on elf x86_64); RED-ON-DISABLE: remove this `includes` field -> the example S0001s on FILE. test_md5.c ADVANCES (its FILE/stdio S0001 class GONE) but is NOT yet CLEAN — a SEPARATE residual fn-ptr->ClientData strict-conversion S0003 (4 sites, Md5_Init passing a function to Tcl_CreateCommand's void* clientData param) is a distinct next-cycle blocker `includes` does not touch.
